title: miliusa filipes
intro: Miliusa filipes, also known as the Chinese dogwood, is a plant species belonging to the Annonaceae family. It is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in tea or infused in oil form to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although I couldn't find any specific information on its common preparations.
